audit_items.proof_trace: match.c calls nfp_tunnel_add_ipv4_off() at L636/L680, which increments a ref_count (and may allocate a new nfp_ipv4_addr_entry via kmalloc) and stores the address in nfp_flow->nfp_tun_ipv4_addr. When nfp_flower_compile_flow_match() subsequently returns an error (e.g., key-length check at L692-695), the caller's err_destroy_flow path in offload.c never calls nfp_tunnel_del_ipv4_off(), leaking the ref_count. This is confirmed by contrast with conntrack.c's ct_offload_err path (L840-841), which correctly calls nfp_tunnel_del_ipv4_off() before freeing the flow.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker with CAP_NET_ADMIN crafts a TC flower rule specifying a VXLAN/GRE IPv4 tunnel endpoint plus enough match fields to exceed NFP_FLOWER_KEY_MAX_LW; each failed offload attempt leaves nfp_ipv4_addr_entry.ref_count permanently incremented, preventing memory reclaim and corrupting the hardware's IPv4 tunnel address list over repeated attempts.
